hard.next_step: 6/22/26 GK: awaiting RFP release first week of July 6/4/26 GK: Leslie response - S2P release pushed to beginning of Q3, we will still be included 5/26/26 GK: follow up with all 3 contacts at C&R, status of SIM award and S2P RFP release 4/13/26 GK: connecting with Checkers team on release date/timeline of S2P RFP 3/30/26 GK: S2P RFP release end of April 3/17/26 GK: followed up regarding SIM integrations and commitment to S2P rfp in following month 3/12/26 GK: connect with SC team - fitment gaps too high, no longer responding to this RFP. but staying engaged with C&R for immiment S2P RFP release in 1 month. sharing integration capabilities with Supplier Incident Mgmt solutions 3/2/26 GK: RFP received. beginning submission and coordinating necessary next steps 2/26/26 GK: demo occurred. rfp release 3/2, plans to release full S2P rfp in May 2026 2/23/26 GK: prep call w/ Srishti Tuesday 2/17/26 GK: scheduling pre-rfp demo for 2/26
hard.ais_why:
• Demo covered Merlin AI assistant, AI-driven supplier onboarding via Smart Onboarding OCR, and QuestGenie scorecard builder - Checkers acknowledged Gartner leadership but did not push AI as a buying criterion.
• The active RFP is scoped to Supplier Incident Management (iSupplier + iRisk), with the full S2P RFP deferred to May; current state is manual Excel scorecarding, not an AI mandate.
• One recorded demo, RFP imminent - solid procurement transformation deal, but AI excitement is Zycus-pitched rather than customer-pulled.

north_star_verdict.headline: Live but stalled in pre-RFP limbo: Zycus is confirmed included in the Checkers & Rally's S2P tender (Leslie, 4 Jun), but the RFP has slipped May to Q3 to first week of July and is now overdue, no economic buyer is engaged, and the 27 Aug close is not credible from Qualified.
north_star_verdict.math: Today 6 Jul 2026. Close 27 Aug 2026 = 52 days out. Qualified since 11 Feb 2026 (about 145 days in stage). Close date pushed twice (25 May to 14 Jul to 27 Aug) tracking a slipping RFP, not progress. Last two-way buyer touch 4 Jun (Leslie confirming inclusion); last logged activity 27 Feb. This is an RFP tender, so the quiet stretch is largely the buyer drafting the spec, but the release is now past its own promised window (first week of July). From Qualified the deal must still see RFP release, submission, evaluation, shortlist, selection, commercials and contracting inside about 7 weeks. Not credible on the current date.
evidence: Leslie confirmed 4 Jun 2026 that S2P release pushed to Q3 and Zycus is still included (SF Next_Step__c).
Rep Next Step 22 Jun 2026: 'awaiting RFP release first week of July' - now overdue as of 6 Jul (SF Next_Step__c).
Demo held 26 Feb 2026; Zycus withdrew from the narrower incident RFP 12 Mar citing fitment gaps (SF Next_Step__c).
Best Case forecast with no economic buyer engaged and no secured champion is indefensible at Qualified; honest category is Pipeline.

metrics.narrative: No quantified value case exists. The 26 Feb demo surfaced qualitative pain only: Josh described that stores 'get a bad product' and log it in 'a web based task management system' where 'if the communication goes off of that specific system generated email, it kinda disappears from the flow', and Leslie confirmed she is 'doing some scorecarding right now with my teams in manual Excel sheets right now until we get a system'. No dollar, headcount, or cycle-time figure was ever stated. In a competitive RFP a quantified cost-of-status-quo is what makes the response score on value, not just fit - build it with Josh before submission.
sources: Avoma demo 26 Feb 2026 (Josh Ullrich, Leslie McElhaney)

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is engaged. The 26 Feb demo was attended by operational contacts (Leslie McElhaney, Josh Ullrich) plus Gina, the most senior voice, who described herself as a former 'head of the commercial organization' and drove the ask to see the full spend-management suite - but Gina dropped off early ('I'm gonna drop off then because I don't need to see all this... when we reconvene, then we can come get me in there'), signalling decisions route up to her and above. No OpportunityContactRole or MEDDPICC field names a budget owner, and no pricing conversation has occurred. The path to power runs through Leslie, the one responsive contact; broker the introduction to the budget owner rather than cold-outreaching on a tender.

decision_criteria.narrative: Partial criteria are visible from the demo. The buyer prioritised supplier governance and incident management, probing configurability ('those are fields that you could customize behind the scenes to fit your needs of what stage one, two, and three') and asking for a weighted KPI scorecard model ('a one to 5 score... put a weighting against and calculate'). Leslie also stated a connected end-to-end requirement ('you wanted a connected end to end system'). A full Source-to-Pay overview covering direct and indirect spend is a stated next-phase criterion. The binding criteria will be set by the RFP itself, which is still unreleased, so the live play is to influence the evaluation framework before it locks.
sources: Avoma demo 26 Feb 2026 (Leslie McElhaney, Josh Ullrich)

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a formal RFP via the Symphony eSourcing system ('you'll all get an email from me before it goes out through Symphony, which is our eSourcing system'). Sequence: pre-RFP demo 26 Feb; a narrower Supplier Incident Management RFP received 2 Mar from which Zycus withdrew 12 Mar citing fitment gaps, while staying engaged for the broader full S2P RFP. That S2P RFP release has slipped May to end of April to Q3 to first week of July. Leslie described a down-select gate ('the folks that are down selected will then do presentations and demos for more of the details'). People engaged are operational; escalation to budget authority has not occurred. The process is buyer-led and paused pending release.
sources: SF Next_Step__c history 2 Mar, 12 Mar, 22 Jun 2026

paper_process.narrative: Some paper mechanics are known: the prospect has signed an NDA ('I know we've signed that NDA'), and procurement runs through the Symphony eSourcing system for RFP distribution and dated response steps. Beyond the NDA and the RFP channel, contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, legal, InfoSec) have not been discussed - normal at Qualified. Visibility will improve once the RFP releases and a shortlist forms; the RFP itself will carry the response dates and steps.

identify_pain.narrative: Operational pain is articulated but not owned at the executive level or quantified. Josh described the current incident flow: restaurants log a bad product in a web task system that emails a predefined chain, and 'if the communication goes off of that specific system generated email, it kinda disappears from the flow'. Leslie confirmed supplier performance is tracked in 'manual Excel sheets right now until we get a system'. The pain owner is the supply-chain and operations team (Josh, Leslie). The financial weight of this pain has not been established with a budget owner, and no compelling event beyond the internal RFP timeline is evident.
sources: Avoma demo 26 Feb 2026 (Josh Ullrich, Leslie McElhaney)

champion.narrative: No secured champion yet, but Leslie McElhaney is the developable candidate: she is the one contact who consistently replies and she proactively confirmed on 4 Jun that Zycus remains included in the S2P RFP - a small but real act of advocacy. She has not introduced Zycus to power, has not shared the competitor shortlist, and has not opened access to a budget owner. What still has to develop is her willingness to broker economic-buyer access and share the evaluation criteria before the RFP scores; that conversion is the relationship priority.

competition.narrative: The competitive field is undisclosed. The buyer is running a formal, competitive RFP via Symphony eSourcing and described a mixed shortlist ('a blend of folks that are companies that are very heavily on FSIM or heavily in the supplier governance space, but they're not true end to end spend management'), but named no rival and Competitors__c is blank. Notably, Zycus already withdrew from the narrower incident RFP on 12 Mar 2026 because 'fitment gaps too high', signalling at least one competitor fits that narrower scope better. For the broader S2P RFP, surfacing who else is shortlisted is essential; this is a genuine knowledge gap, not a no-competitor situation.

deal_movement.summary: Momentum is rep-driven and thinning. Since the 26 Feb demo no buyer-facing deliverable has been executed; Grace has chased status on 13 Apr, 26 May and 22 Jun, and the last two-way buyer touch was Leslie's 4 Jun confirmation of inclusion. The RFP release and the close date have both slipped repeatedly, tracking the buyer's internal drafting rather than deal progress.

items.change: Pre-RFP demo of iSupplier / iRisk supplier governance and incident management delivered to Leslie, Josh and Gina.
Narrower Supplier Incident Management RFP received; Zycus began submission.
Zycus withdrew from the narrow Supplier Incident Management RFP citing fitment gaps too high, staying engaged for the broader S2P RFP.
Leslie confirmed S2P release pushed to beginning of Q3 and Zycus remains included.
Close date pushed 14 Jul to 27 Aug, tracking the slipping RFP.
Rep note: awaiting RFP release first week of July (now overdue).

competitive_position.summary: Competition is undisclosed. The buyer is running a formal Symphony eSourcing RFP against a mixed shortlist of supplier-governance / FSIM specialists and end-to-end spend-management suites (Leslie, 26 Feb), so rivals exist but none is named and Competitors__c is blank. Zycus's own withdrawal from the narrower incident-management RFP on 12 Mar (fitment gaps) implies at least one competitor fits that narrower scope better. The single most useful competitive move is to get Leslie to disclose the S2P shortlist and evaluation weighting before the spec locks, to avoid a second fitment loss on the broader scope.

competitors.name: Undisclosed RFP field
competitors.quote: fitment gaps too high, no longer responding to this RFP
competitors.how_we_win: Get Leslie to disclose the S2P shortlist and evaluation weighting, then lead with iSupplier governance plus Merlin Intake and connected end-to-end S2P differentiation the incumbent manual process and point FSIM tools lack.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Zycus is aligned on configurable incident management and KPI scorecarding, partially aligned on the connected end-to-end S2P ask (full suite is Zycus's strength but the current opp scope is only iSupplier / iRisk), and exposed on narrow Supplier Incident Management fit, where Zycus already withdrew citing gaps.

items.quote: Completely dynamic. So one thing that Zycus does... dynamic workflows and routing logic

items.criterion: Weighted KPI scorecarding on a scored rubric
items.quote: I see your calculate by average, but then the scorecarding aligned to a 1 to 5 score, put a weighting against and calculate

items.quote: the p to p piece and the direct and indirect spend is not getting covered today

items.criterion: Fit for the narrower Supplier Incident Management RFP

best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers matter most now: convert Leslie into an active champion, multi-thread to the budget owner before the RFP scores, and shape the S2P spec after the narrow-scope fitment loss.
flags: Convert Leslie from inclusion-confirmer into an active champion who shares the evaluation criteria and shortlist and brokers budget-owner access before the RFP scores.
Multi-thread beyond operations (Leslie, Josh, Gina) to the economic buyer; a Best Case tender with no power access is exposed and single-threaded.
Influence the S2P RFP spec while it is still being drafted; after the 12 Mar fitment loss on the narrow SIM RFP, shape requirements to Zycus's connected end-to-end and Merlin strengths.

items.name: Leslie McElhaney
items.risk: Most responsive contact and the developable champion; proactively confirmed Zycus inclusion 4 Jun but has no proven access to budget authority and has not shared the shortlist.
items.source: Avoma demo 26 Feb 2026 + SF Next_Step__c 4 Jun 2026

items.name: Gina
items.risk: Most senior buyer voice and closest to power (self-described former commercial-org head) and drove the full-suite ask, but dropped off the 26 Feb demo and is now passive; re-engaging her is the power path.

items.name: Josh Ullrich
items.risk: Owns the operational incident-management pain and store-level workflow; a technical evaluator, not yet advocating.

champion_strength.summary: No confirmed champion, but Leslie McElhaney is the developable candidate: she is the only consistently responsive contact and proactively confirmed Zycus's continued inclusion in the S2P RFP on 4 Jun. She has not yet opened access to power or shared competitive intel. Develop her into an active sponsor who brokers economic-buyer access before the RFP scores.
champion_strength.source: Avoma demo 26 Feb 2026 + SF Next_Step__c 4 Jun 2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus led hard on AI in the 26 Feb demo (Merlin agentic AI, Smart Onboarding OCR, QuestGenie scorecard builder), but the buyer acknowledged Gartner leadership without pulling AI as a buying criterion. The agentic story is Zycus-pitched, not customer-pulled, so it is not yet a differentiator on this deal.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 4.0 / Moderate. The demo showcased Merlin, OCR onboarding and QuestGenie, and the buyer acknowledged Zycus's 2025 Gartner Magic Quadrant leadership, but framed the need as replacing manual Excel scorecarding rather than an AI mandate. AI is curiosity, not a stated criterion.

items.detail: No economic buyer or budget owner engaged; deal lives with operational contacts and the one senior voice (Gina) is passive after dropping off the demo.
S2P RFP release has slipped May to end of April to Q3 to first week of July and is now overdue; close date pushed twice to 27 Aug.
Zycus withdrew from the narrow Supplier Incident Management RFP on 12 Mar citing fitment gaps, a signal the same gap could recur on the broader S2P scope if the spec is not shaped.
Single-threaded into operations (Leslie, Josh); if Leslie disengages there is no other live relationship or path to power.
items.source: Avoma demo 26 Feb 2026 + SF Next_Step__c 4 Jun 2026

items.detail: No budget confirmed and no pricing conversation held; a $125k Best Case forecast rests on no verified funding at Qualified.
items.source: Avoma demo 26 Feb 2026 + SF field history

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cooling: the last two-way buyer exchange was Leslie's 4 Jun note, and every touch since has been rep-side chasing a repeatedly-slipping RFP. The main threat is buyer inertia (an internal RFP that keeps slipping as the team juggles multiple sourcing projects), not a named rival.
items: Last two-way buyer touch 4 Jun 2026; rep-only chasing since.
RFP release overdue against its own first-week-of-July promise.
Gina, the senior stakeholder, disengaged from the demo and has not returned.

items.action: Contact Leslie, Josh and Gina to confirm the S2P RFP release and secure its spec and timeline.
items.trigger: Rep note awaiting RFP release first week of July, now overdue
items.expected_effect: Locks the tender timeline and prevents the deal stalling further in pre-RFP limbo.

items.action: Ask Leslie to share the evaluation criteria and shortlist and introduce the budget owner.
items.trigger: No economic buyer engaged; Leslie the only responsive contact
items.expected_effect: Opens access to power and lets Zycus shape the scoring framework before it locks.

items.action: Work with Josh to quantify manual scorecarding and lost-incident costs for the RFP response.
items.trigger: Buyer described manual Excel scorecards and incidents lost off the email chain
items.expected_effect: Gives the RFP response a value case, not just fit, raising the win position.

items.action: Get Gina onto a live full-suite S2P walkthrough after she reviews the recorded overview.
items.trigger: Gina wanted the full spend-management suite but dropped off the demo
items.expected_effect: Re-engages the senior stakeholder and warms the larger S2P phase ahead of the RFP.

critical_signals.text: The tender field is undisclosed, and Zycus already withdrew from the narrower incident-management RFP on 12 March citing fitment gaps, so a rival fits that scope better.
The buyer wants a connected end-to-end Source-to-Pay suite covering direct and indirect spend, well beyond the current iSupplier and Merlin Intake scope.
Gina, the most senior buyer voice and the one who drove the full-suite ask, dropped off the 26 February demo and remains unengaged.
The 26 February demo drifted off the incident-management agenda and closed with only a promise to send a recorded overview, not a committed next step.
A $125k Best Case rests on no engaged budget owner and no pricing discussion, and the deciding RFP keeps slipping.

scope_change.detail: Opp scope is unchanged; the buyer signals a larger future full S2P phase (direct and indirect spend) as a separate, bigger lift, so appetite is expanding rather than the current scope narrowing.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Zycus is confirmed included in the Checkers & Rally's S2P tender (Leslie, 4 Jun) but the deciding RFP has slipped May to Q3 to first week of July and is now overdue; with no economic buyer engaged and no champion secured from Qualified, a 27 Aug close is not credible.

win_position.text: Score sits low because the deal is still Qualified after about 145 days with the deciding RFP unreleased, so there is no evaluation, shortlist or commercial motion yet to earn a higher number (SF stage + Next_Step, 22 Jun).
No economic buyer is engaged: the 26 Feb demo drew only operational contacts and the one senior voice, Gina, dropped off before the demo, so budget authority is unreachable today (Avoma, 26 Feb).

Zycus already withdrew from the narrower Supplier Incident Management RFP on 12 Mar citing fitment gaps, so a rival fits that scope better and the broader S2P field is still undisclosed (SF Next_Step, 12 Mar).
One genuine positive: the buyer acknowledged Zycus's 2025 Gartner Magic Quadrant leadership and Gina asked to revisit the full S2P suite she was impressed by years ago (Avoma, 26 Feb).

deal_momentum.text: RFP release has slipped from May to end of April to Q3 to first week of July and is now overdue, with no buyer-facing deliverable executed since the 26 Feb demo (SF Next_Step history).
The close date has been pushed twice, 25 May to 14 Jul to 27 Aug, tracking the slipping RFP rather than real progress (SF field history, 11 May and 15 Jun).
Momentum is rep-driven: Grace chased status on 13 Apr, 26 May and 22 Jun, and the last two-way buyer touch was Leslie's 4 Jun note confirming inclusion.

customer_commitment.text: No economic buyer or budget owner is mapped; the deal lives with operations (Leslie, Josh) while Gina stays passive after dropping off the demo (Avoma, 26 Feb).
The buyer committed only to keep Zycus in the tender and to review a recorded full-suite overview, not to any pricing, POC or decision step (Leslie, 4 Jun).
The Best Case forecast is indefensible at Qualified with no champion and no economic buyer; the honest category is Pipeline.

deal_risk.text: Inertia is the main threat: a repeatedly-slipping internal RFP could stall indefinitely as the buyer juggles multiple concurrent sourcing projects (Josh and Leslie, 26 Feb).
A second fitment loss looms; Zycus lost the narrow SIM RFP on fit, and without shaping the S2P spec the same gap could recur (SF Next_Step, 12 Mar).
The deal is single-threaded into operations with no power access, so if Leslie disengages there is no other live relationship (Avoma 26 Feb + Next_Step 4 Jun).

deal_momentum.evidence: RFP overdue and slipped three times; no buyer deliverable since 26 Feb; close date pushed twice.

customer_commitment.evidence: No EB mapped; buyer committed only to inclusion and reviewing a video, no pricing or decision step.

deal_risk.evidence: Inertia on a slipping internal RFP, prior fitment loss risk, and single-thread with no power access.

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer acknowledged Zycus's Gartner leadership but asked mainly to 'send that video' of the full suite and to 'look at schedules'; no Zycus-specific commercial next step, and Gina dropped off the demo (Avoma, 26 Feb).

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain only: manual Excel scorecarding and incident emails that 'kinda disappear from the flow'; no cost, headcount or cycle-time figure stated (Avoma, 26 Feb).

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 15 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

commentary.win_position: Win 10 is carried by competitive strength +0.20 (weight 15); business case strength +0.30 (weight 10). Offsetting it: preference strength -0.50 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 49 (roughly flat): Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (10), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 49, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

cro_panel.intro: Zycus is confirmed included in the Checkers & Rally's S2P tender (Leslie, 4 Jun) but the deciding RFP has slipped May to Q3 to first week of July and is now overdue; with no economic buyer engaged and no champion secured from Qualified, a 27 Aug close is not credible.

bullets.text: Inertia is the main threat: a repeatedly-slipping internal RFP could stall indefinitely as the buyer juggles multiple concurrent sourcing projects (Josh and Leslie, 26 Feb).
A second fitment loss looms; Zycus lost the narrow SIM RFP on fit, and without shaping the S2P spec the same gap could recur (SF Next_Step, 12 Mar).
The deal is single-threaded into operations with no power access, so if Leslie disengages there is no other live relationship (Avoma 26 Feb + Next_Step 4 Jun).
